what: Self-improving autoresearch: the harness (or a coding agent driving it) autonomously improves the megakernel over AMK's own default schedule, correctness-gated and drift-robust.
search_space: COMBINED candidate = ScheduleConfig (tiling/fusion/sm/pipelining/page/threads/smem) + kernel_knobs (cols_per_warp, cpasync, cpa_stages, cpa_cols). The kernel knobs are the MegakernelVM compile-time GEMV build knobs; the ScheduleConfig's N_tile + threads_per_block are the schedule-side levers that also move measured latency.
fitness: CUDA-event median (warmup 25, iters 100), correctness-gated vs the CPU ReferenceVM at the model's native dtype tolerance; keep/revert decided by INTERLEAVED back-to-back A/B re-measurement of candidate vs resident incumbent (cancels WDDM clock drift); >2% margin to keep.
honesty_guards: no latency without a correctness PASS
physical-floor guard: any latency below weights/HBM-bandwidth floor is withheld as an artifact (caught a silent infeasible-launch / cost-model fallback at 87.6us < 695us floor)
drift-robust interleaved keep/revert (a candidate reading lower standalone is reverted if same-clock it is not >2% faster)
model: small bf16 (hidden2048/4L/GQA inter5632/vocab32000), 622 MB weights, RTX 5090 Laptop sm_120

agent_in_the_loop.driver: a coding agent (Claude) hand-forming candidates from the decode playbook, driving amk eval + amk_orchestrate record
agent_in_the_loop.method: 7 interleaved A/B rounds; median baseline 2514us -> median best 1991us
agent_in_the_loop.note: single-shot 1.51x was a favorable-thermal-moment reading; the drift-robust interleaved 1.25x is the number we report

autoresearch_combined_cold_warm.note: within-run cold speedup is drift-free; cross-run absolute carries WDDM drift (flagged). Warm = flywheel corpus seed.

overnight.mode: amk autoresearch small --gpu rtx5090 --minutes M --device cuda --overnight
overnight.headline_speedup: TO FINALIZE from the completed 2h campaign (workspace/overnight_small_state.json)

honest_scope: Every speedup here is vs AMK's OWN default schedule, measured and correctness-gated. It is NOT a claim of beating cuBLAS/vLLM: at batch-1 AMK is behind those (it carries the megakernel's DAG-dispatch + cross-SM counter-sync a single library GEMV does not). The self-improvement loop recovers headroom WITHIN the megakernel design; closing the gap to cuBLAS needs new Layer-1 micro-kernels (tensor-core/DP4A GEMV), which the same loop will then find and keep.
reproduce: agent/manual: amk_cli.py eval small --gpu rtx5090 --config cfg.json --device cuda  (+ amk_orchestrate record)
headless: amk_cli.py autoresearch small --gpu rtx5090 --minutes 120 --device cuda --overnight
